title: INDIA-JAPAN: Educational Exchange Program
content: Event Date: 9th February 2024 The School of Liberal Education at Galgotias University had the distinct honor of hosting a momentous Special Invited Talk on the "INDIA-JAPAN: Educational Exchange Program." This event, held on 09-02-24, was a testament to the collaborative endeavors between India and Japan, focusing on the enhancement of cultural exchange and academic collaboration and was a part of the MOU signed with Nihonkai. The distinguished Japanese delegates, Mr. Takashi Emoto, International Director, and Mr. Takeshi Kuga, Assistant International Director, both representing Shikoku University, Japan, along with Ms. Ashima from Nihonkai, graced the occasion with their esteemed presence. Their insights provided a comprehensive understanding of the educational landscape in Japan, fostering a deeper appreciation for the bilateral collaboration between the two nations. The event commenced with a welcome address by Dr. Anuradha Parasar, Dean of the School of Liberal Education at Galgotias University. Dr. Parasar underscored the significance of such exchanges in promoting global perspectives and enriching the overall learning experience for students. She emphasized the importance of cultural immersion and academic engagement in preparing students for the interconnected world. The talk delved into the specifics of the educational exchange program, shedding light on the myriad opportunities it offers for students to engage in cross-cultural experiences and academic pursuits. The delegates shared success stories and testimonials from previous collaborations, illustrating the tangible benefits of such programs in nurturing well-rounded global citizens. Noteworthy interactions took place between the Japanese delegates and the leadership of Galgotias University. The Honorable Chancellor Mr. Sunil Galgotia, Vice-Chancellor Prof. (Dr.) Mallikharjuna Babu and Director of HR Ms. Shilpi Chandra actively engaged with the delegates, discussing potential avenues for future collaborations and expressing their commitment to strengthening educational ties between the two nations. The synergy between the leadership of Galgotias University and the Japanese delegates underscored a shared vision for fostering international partnerships and promoting academic excellence. One of the highlights of the event was the interactive session that allowed for a meaningful exchange of ideas and perspectives between the delegates and the attendees, which included faculty members and students from the School of Liberal Education. The students, in particular, benefited from the first-hand experiences shared by the Japanese delegates, gaining valuable insights into the cultural nuances and academic opportunities in Japan. The engagement was not only educational but also served to create a sense of unity and cooperation among the participants. Post-event, the delegates had the opportunity to interact with students individually, addressing queries and providing guidance on potential study and research opportunities in Japan. This personalized interaction further enriched the overall experience for the attendees, fostering a sense of mentorship and collaboration. In conclusion, the Special Invited Talk on the "INDIA-JAPAN: Educational Exchange Program" at Galgotias University was a resounding success, deepening the ties between the two nations in the realm of education. The commitment of the university leadership, the active participation of the School of Liberal Education, and the invaluable contributions of the Japanese delegates collectively contributed to an event that not only celebrated existing collaborations but also laid the foundation for future endeavors. The School of Liberal Education at Galgotias University looks forward to building upon this success and furthering initiatives that strengthen the educational bonds between India and Japan. The event was successfully coordinated by Dr. Shadab Ahamad Ansari, School of Liberal Education, Galgotias University. Mentor Name – Prof. Dr. Anuradha Parasar, Dean, School of Liberal Education Department Name – School of Liberal Education
